claims.summary: Cautilus is a framework for discovering, evaluating, and improving agent behavior.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` is the framework for discovering, evaluating, and improving agent behavior.

claims.edgeRationale: The three jobs name discovery (D1-discovery), evaluation (E1-evaluate), and improvement (I1-bounded-improvement) under the apex behavior-honesty promise (APEX).

claims.summary: Cautilus lets you pin down the behavior that matters and prove it survives every change to prompts, skills, and models.

excerpts.verbatim: It lets you pin down the behavior that matters, prove it survives every change to your prompts, skills, and models, and improve it within explicit budgets—whether you're protecting an `AGENTS.md`, a single skill, a prompt, or a full agent loop.

claims.edgeRationale: Pinning behavior that survives change is the apex promise (APEX); improving within explicit budgets supports bounded improvement (I1-bounded-improvement).

claims.summary: The three jobs connect: discover declared behavior claims worth proving from selected source docs, verify curated claims through bounded evaluation packets, and improve behavior once the proof surface is honest.

excerpts.verbatim: The three jobs connect: discover the declared behavior claims worth proving from selected source docs, verify the curated claims through bounded evaluation packets, and improve behavior once the proof surface is honest.

claims.edgeRationale: Discover maps to D1-discovery, verify through bounded evaluation packets to E1-evaluate, and improve to I1-bounded-improvement, all under the apex connection (APEX).

claims.summary: Cautilus ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent that a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` ships as a standalone binary plus Cautilus Agent, which a host repo can install without copying another scaffold first.

claims.summary: Agents are first-class users of the product surface.

excerpts.verbatim: Agents are first-class users of the product surface.

claims.edgeRationale: Agent-first design grounds the agent orchestration epic (A1-orchestration) and the apex product stance that agents drive the workflow (APEX).

claims.summary: Commands emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.

excerpts.verbatim: Commands should emit durable packets with enough state for the next agent to resume, not only terminal prose for a human operator.

claims.edgeRationale: Resumable durable packets serve agent orchestration handoff (A1-orchestration) and the apex packet-first reopenable-evidence stance (APEX).

claims.summary: Cautilus installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` installs as a machine-level binary, but its agent-facing surface is intentionally repo-local.

claims.summary: The binary is shared across repos.

excerpts.verbatim: The binary is shared across repos.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and instruction-routing surface are not shared across repos; they stay checked into each host repo.

excerpts.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent surface, adapter wiring, prompts, and instruction-routing surface are not.
They stay checked into each host repo so evaluation behavior remains reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the repo that declares it.

claims.edgeRationale: Repo-local checked-in agent surface is an install/ownership boundary (S1-install) that also makes evaluation behavior reproducible and owned by the declaring repo (M1-proven-on-itself).

claims.summary: Checked-in repo-local evaluation surface keeps evaluation behavior reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the repo that declares it.

excerpts.verbatim: They stay checked into each host repo so evaluation behavior remains reproducible, reviewable, and owned by the repo that declares it.

claims.edgeRationale: Reproducible/reviewable/owned evaluation behavior is a proven-on-itself property (M1-proven-on-itself) conferred by the repo-local install boundary (S1-install).

claims.summary: Cautilus proves its own promises with honest badges: readiness and claim discovery run live in executable specs (proven), behavior evaluation, bounded improvement, reviewable artifacts, and host ownership project saved evidence bundles (declared), and a testable-agent runner is promised.

excerpts.verbatim: Cautilus proves its own promises with honest badges ([the apex spec](./docs/specs/index.spec.md)): readiness and claim discovery run live in executable specs (**proven**), while behavior evaluation, bounded improvement, reviewable artifacts, and host ownership project saved evidence bundles (**declared**) and a testable-agent runner is **promised**.

claims.edgeRationale: Honest badge proof is the proven-on-itself promise (M1-proven-on-itself) covering the apex (APEX), with readiness (S2-readiness) and claim discovery (D1-discovery) called out as the live/proven slices.

claims.summary: For cross-repo adoption, the bounded evaluation loop is the most ready slice: host repos can use cautilus evaluate fixture, cautilus evaluate observation, and post-run cautilus evaluate skill-experiment with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.

excerpts.verbatim: For cross-repo adoption, the bounded evaluation loop is the most ready slice: host repos can use `cautilus evaluate fixture`, `cautilus evaluate observation`, and post-run `cautilus evaluate skill-experiment` with checked-in fixtures, host-owned adapters, preserved task packets, and the current evaluation and skill-experiment report packets.

claims.edgeRationale: Evaluate fixture/observation is the core evaluate loop (E1-evaluate), and skill-experiment is the scenario/experiment compare surface (E2-scenarios-experiments).

claims.summary: skill-experiment compare compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs; it does not clone, install, or execute skills.

excerpts.verbatim: `skill-experiment compare` compares host-preserved baseline and variant outputs; it does not clone, install, or execute skills.

claims.summary: Claim discovery and bounded improvement ship today and are opt-in for host repos that adopt them.

excerpts.verbatim: Claim discovery and bounded improvement ship today and are opt-in for host repos that adopt them.

claims.edgeRationale: Claim discovery ships under D1-discovery and bounded improvement ships under I1-bounded-improvement, both opt-in.

claims.summary: Cautilus is not for repos that only need deterministic lint, unit, or type checks and do not have an evaluator-dependent behavior surface.

excerpts.verbatim: Not for: repos that only need deterministic lint, unit, or type checks and do not have an evaluator-dependent behavior surface.

claims.summary: Cautilus runs on native macOS or native Linux, requires a target host repo editable locally, and requires git available on PATH.

excerpts.verbatim: - native macOS or native Linux
- `git` available on `PATH`

claims.edgeRationale: Platform and git prerequisites gate install (S1-install) and are part of first-run readiness (S2-readiness).

claims.summary: Cautilus installs via the install.sh curl script and cautilus init in a host repo.

excerpts.verbatim: cautilus init
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/corca-ai/cautilus/main/install.sh

claims.summary: Setup can be handed to an agent instead of running the install steps yourself.

excerpts.verbatim: You can also hand setup to an agent instead of running these steps yourself.

claims.edgeRationale: Agent-driven setup is part of install (S1-install) carried out by the orchestrating agent (A1-orchestration).

claims.summary: Raw discover claims packets are the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.

excerpts.verbatim: Raw `discover claims` packets remain the high-recall, source-ref-backed proof-planning input, not the primary document a user should review.

claims.edgeRationale: Raw discovery packets are the discovery output (D1-discovery) that the agent curates before review (A2-curation-review).

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates the discovery packet against the repo: reduce false positives, raise likely missing public promises, and separate in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.

excerpts.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent curates that packet against the repo: reduce false positives, raise likely missing public promises, and separate in-scope discovery bugs from out-of-scope narrative gaps.

claims.edgeRationale: Agent curation is the curation/review epic (A2-curation-review) operating on discovery output (D1-discovery).

claims.summary: The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree, but host repos do not need that renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.

excerpts.verbatim: The public website report is generated from the claim spec tree, but host repos do not need that renderer before Cautilus can inspect readiness, claims, evals, or improvement work.

claims.edgeRationale: The generated website report serves proven-on-itself reporting (M1-proven-on-itself), while the boundary that the renderer is not required for inspection supports readiness/onboarding (S2-readiness).

claims.summary: Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

excerpts.verbatim: Each claim page pairs a bounded product promise with executable evidence or an explicit evidence gap.

claims.summary: The bounded eval loop needs one checked-in cautilus.evaluation_input.v1 fixture and a host-owned adapter runner.

excerpts.verbatim: You need one checked-in `cautilus.evaluation_input.v1` fixture and a host-owned adapter runner.

claims.summary: The bounded eval loop verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.

excerpts.verbatim: This loop verifies a bounded behavior fixture and produces reopenable observed and summary packets.

claims.summary: Cautilus turns the fixture run into durable eval packets that another agent or maintainer can reopen.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` turns the fixture run into durable eval packets that another agent or maintainer can reopen.

claims.summary: The eval summary is not a global product verdict; it is evidence for the behavior fixture and adapter path that the host repo chose.

excerpts.verbatim: The summary is not a global product verdict; it is evidence for the behavior fixture and adapter path that the host repo chose.

claims.summary: After a fixture run, a human decides whether the evidence is enough for the host repo's current proof need.

excerpts.verbatim: Next step: a human decides whether that evidence is enough for the host repo's current proof need.

claims.summary: Cautilus turns behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface.

excerpts.verbatim: It is the shortest currently stable external-adoption example of the product claim: `Cautilus` turns behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface.

claims.edgeRationale: Turning behavior evidence into a reviewable decision surface is the apex product claim (APEX) realized by the evaluate loop (E1-evaluate).

claims.summary: In charness, an instruction-surface fixture proved that the agent first selected the startup bootstrap helper find-skills, then selected the durable work skill for the actual task.

excerpts.verbatim: In `charness`, an instruction-surface fixture proved that the agent first selected the startup bootstrap helper `find-skills`, then selected the durable work skill for the actual task.

claims.edgeRationale: The charness dogfood fixture is self-proof evidence (M1-proven-on-itself) produced through the evaluate fixture loop (E1-evaluate).

claims.summary: The dogfood run turned the question of whether the agent read and followed the repo instructions from transcript judgment into a reproducible packet with artifacts another maintainer can reopen.

excerpts.verbatim: That turned "did the agent read and follow the repo instructions?" from transcript judgment into a reproducible packet with artifacts another maintainer can reopen.

claims.edgeRationale: Turning transcript judgment into a reproducible reopenable packet is the evaluate-loop value (E1-evaluate) demonstrated as self-proof (M1-proven-on-itself).

claims.summary: The dogfood run exposed that routing proof is not backend subagent capability proof, and keeping that distinction in the packet prevented over-claiming what had been verified.

excerpts.verbatim: The same dogfood run also exposed a useful limit: routing proof is not backend subagent capability proof.
Keeping that distinction in the packet prevented the result from over-claiming what had been verified.

claims.edgeRationale: Preserving the scope-of-proof distinction is an honest self-proof discipline (M1-proven-on-itself) recorded in the eval packet (E1-evaluate).

claims.summary: Cautilus has three connected product layers: claim discovery, bounded evaluation, and bounded improvement.

excerpts.verbatim: Cautilus has three connected product layers:
claim discovery, bounded evaluation, and bounded improvement.

claims.edgeRationale: The three layers are claim discovery (D1-discovery), bounded evaluation (E1-evaluate), and bounded improvement (I1-bounded-improvement) under the apex product shape (APEX).

claims.summary: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked docs into cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1 candidates.

excerpts.verbatim: Claim discovery turns adapter-owned entry docs and linked docs into `cautilus.claim_proof_plan.v1` candidates.

claims.summary: Claim discovery is proof planning, not a verdict that the repo is correct.

excerpts.verbatim: It is proof planning, not a verdict that the repo is correct.

claims.summary: The Cautilus Agent curates false positives, likely missing promises, scan boundaries, and extraction and review budgets before any eval plan is trusted.

excerpts.verbatim: The Cautilus Agent curates false positives, likely missing promises, scan boundaries, and extraction and review budgets before any eval plan is trusted.

claims.edgeRationale: Agent curation before trust is the curation/review epic (A2-curation-review) gating the discovery output (D1-discovery).

claims.summary: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: dev for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and app for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.

excerpts.verbatim: Evaluation uses two top-level surfaces: `dev` for AI-assisted development work such as repo contracts, tools, and skills, and `app` for AI-powered product behavior such as chat, prompt, and service responses.

claims.edgeRationale: The dev/app surface split structures the core evaluate surface (E1-evaluate) across which scenarios and experiments are run (E2-scenarios-experiments).

claims.summary: Sample normalization inputs live in examples/starters/ and the checked-in fixture directories under fixtures/.

excerpts.verbatim: Sample normalization inputs live in [examples/starters/](./examples/starters/) and the checked-in fixture directories under [fixtures/](./fixtures/).

claims.summary: Cautilus treats a context-recovery case as a protected scenario kept out of tuning so the signal stays honest.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` treats the context-recovery case as a protected scenario kept out of tuning so the signal stays honest.

claims.edgeRationale: Holding a scenario out of tuning is the bounded-improvement held-out discipline (I1-bounded-improvement) over a protected scenario (E2-scenarios-experiments).

claims.summary: Cautilus stores the evidence in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.

excerpts.verbatim: It stores the evidence in a durable file the next maintainer can reopen from disk.

claims.edgeRationale: Durable reopenable evidence files are an evaluate-loop output (E1-evaluate) embodying the apex packet-first stance (APEX).

claims.summary: Cautilus treats packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions as agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a human-readable mirror, not the source of truth.

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a dashboard-first review tool, `Cautilus` treats packets, CLI commands, and repo instructions as agent-facing interfaces first; HTML is a human-readable mirror, not the source of truth.

claims.edgeRationale: Agent-facing-first interfaces with HTML as a mirror is an ownership/source-of-truth boundary supporting agent orchestration (A1-orchestration) and the apex packet-first stance (APEX).

claims.summary: Cautilus does not freeze one prompt string as the contract; it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (intent-first).

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a prompt manager, `Cautilus` does not freeze one prompt string as the contract — it treats the behavior under evaluation as the contract (`intent-first`).

claims.summary: Cautilus separates iteration from protected validation and keeps evidence reopenable from files (held-out honesty, packet-first).

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike a benchmark scrapbook, `Cautilus` separates iteration from protected validation and keeps evidence reopenable from files (`held-out honesty`, `packet-first`).

claims.edgeRationale: Separating iteration from protected validation is the held-out improvement discipline (I1-bounded-improvement); keeping evidence reopenable from files is the packet-first evaluate property (E1-evaluate).

claims.summary: Cautilus makes adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts first-class product boundaries (structured review).

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike ad hoc eval scripts, `Cautilus` makes adapters, reports, review files, and compare artifacts first-class product boundaries (`structured review`).

claims.edgeRationale: First-class adapters/reports/review/compare artifacts is the structured-review boundary (A2-curation-review) that compare artifacts under experiments rely on (E2-scenarios-experiments).

claims.summary: Cautilus keeps search and revision explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions (bounded autonomy).

excerpts.verbatim: Unlike open-ended improver loops, `Cautilus` keeps search and revision explicitly bounded by budgets, checkpoints, and blocked-readiness conditions (`bounded autonomy`).

claims.summary: Human-auditable claims stay readable, deterministic claims belong in ordinary tests and CI, evaluator-dependent behavior goes through cautilus evaluate, and improvement work waits until the proof surface is explicit.

excerpts.verbatim: Human-auditable claims stay readable.
Deterministic claims belong in ordinary tests and CI.
Evaluator-dependent behavior goes through `cautilus evaluate`.
Improvement work waits until the proof surface is explicit.

claims.edgeRationale: Splitting proof layers is an apex design principle (APEX) that routes evaluator-dependent behavior through evaluate (E1-evaluate) and gates improvement on an explicit proof surface (I1-bounded-improvement).

claims.summary: Cautilus ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver: multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.

excerpts.verbatim: `Cautilus` also ships a GEPA-style bounded prompt search seam above the one-shot improver: multi-generation reflective mutation, protected reevaluation, frontier-promotion review reuse, checkpoint feedback reinjection, bounded merge synthesis, and Pareto-style frontier selection.

claims.summary: Two entry points share one host-owned cautilus-adapter.yaml and return the same durable decision surface.

excerpts.verbatim: Two entry points share one host-owned `cautilus-adapter.yaml` and return the same durable decision surface.

claims.edgeRationale: Two entry points sharing one host-owned adapter is an orchestration boundary (A1-orchestration) grounded in the host-owned install surface (S1-install).

claims.summary: Operators use the standalone CLI; Claude and Codex use the repo-local Cautilus Agent that cautilus init installs under .agents/skills/cautilus-agent/.

excerpts.verbatim: Operators use the standalone CLI.
Claude and Codex use the repo-local Cautilus Agent that `cautilus init` installs under `.agents/skills/cautilus-agent/`.

claims.edgeRationale: Init installing the repo-local agent under a fixed path is an install fact (S1-install) that provisions the agent orchestration surface (A1-orchestration).

claims.summary: The minimum host-repo shape is an adapter, an installed Cautilus Agent, and run artifacts such as eval-cases.json, eval-observed.json, and eval-summary.json.

excerpts.verbatim: The minimum host-repo shape is an adapter, an installed Cautilus Agent, and run artifacts such as `eval-cases.json`, `eval-observed.json`, and `eval-summary.json`.

claims.edgeRationale: The minimum host-repo shape defines the install footprint (S1-install) and what doctor checks for readiness (S2-readiness).

claims.summary: The result is not just a pass/fail bit; it is a set of machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen.

excerpts.verbatim: The result is not just a pass/fail bit: it is a set of machine-readable packets plus readable views that another maintainer or agent can reopen.

claims.edgeRationale: Machine-readable packets plus reopenable readable views are the evaluate decision surface (E1-evaluate) consumed during structured review (A2-curation-review).

claims.summary: cautilus doctor --next-action reports the next onboarding step, cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface reports agent-surface discoverability, and cautilus doctor reports repo wiring readiness.

excerpts.verbatim: Use `cautilus doctor --next-action` for the next onboarding step, `cautilus doctor --scope agent-surface` for agent-surface discoverability, and `cautilus doctor` for repo wiring readiness.

claims.summary: From this repo, npm run consumer:onboard:smoke is an end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.

excerpts.verbatim: From this repo, `npm run consumer:onboard:smoke` is an end-to-end adoption proof against a fresh consumer.

claims.edgeRationale: An end-to-end fresh-consumer onboard smoke is self-proof evidence (M1-proven-on-itself) of the install/adoption path (S1-install).
